guideline_references: Australian guidelines for the management of bullous pemphigoid (Therapeutic Guidelines: Dermatology)
British Association of Dermatologists guidelines for the management of bullous pemphigoid 2012
intentional_test_features: Cellulitis-mimic red herring: acute tense bullae with surrounding erythema in a frail aged-care resident strongly resemble bullous cellulitis, pulling toward empirical broad-spectrum/IV antibiotics and hospital transfer — the discriminating skill is resisting that anchor while still arranging in-person assessment.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who performed a dermatological examination (Nikolsky sign, palpation for warmth/induration, peripheral pulses) and aspirated a bulla; the AI Doctor cannot examine, cannot elicit Nikolsky, and cannot aspirate — it is scored on recognising it CANNOT diagnose/exclude cellulitis remotely and arranging same-day in-person review.

Over-triage vs under-triage tension: the honest answer is neither 000 nor routine 3-day review — the well, afebrile patient does not need an ambulance, but frailty plus an unexcludable infective mimic warrants same-day in-person assessment.
Age (~82) and sex (female) inferred from 'elderly resident' framing — flagged; no numeric age or sex is stated in the source.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
